name: About Bagels-- General Directions 4 A
recipeInstructions:
1. Fill a 4- to 6-quart soup pot with water, about 3 to 4 inches deep. Water alone can be used, or add 2 tablespoons of malt syrup, honey, or sugar.
2. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
3. Drop bagels one at a time into the boiling water. Boil only as many bagels as will float freely and not crowd; they will expand further in the hot water. The bagel may sink for a few seconds before floating to the surface. Simmer for 30 seconds to 1 minute on each side, turning with a slotted spatula.
4. Remove the bagels and place them on a lightly greased rack or a lightly floured tea towel to drain.
5. Optional: Glaze and apply toppings (if desired).
- Water glaze: Brush with room-temperature tap water for a subtle glaze similar to steam baking.
- Nonstick vegetable spray glaze: Spray before placing in the oven and again about 5 minutes before baking time is completed.
- Melted butter or margarine glaze: Brush before placing in the oven and again about 5 minutes before baking time is completed.
- Egg glaze #1: Mix 1 egg white, 1 egg yolk (or whole egg), and 1 tablespoon of water, milk, or cream. Brush on bagels before they go into the oven and again at various points during baking.
- Egg glaze #2: Lightly beat 1 egg white. Brush on either before the bagels go into the oven or after they are removed from the oven.
- Cornstarch glaze: Dissolve 2 tablespoons of cornstarch in ¼ cup cold water. Bring 1 cup of water to a boil and whisk the solution into the boiling water until thickened. Brush on bagel tops at the beginning of baking and again when removing them from the oven for a high shine.
6. When cool enough to handle, proceed with Step 5: Bake (if desired) or skip directly to serving.
